summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC testing framework in Firefox have focused on improving the reliability and coverage of tests for macOS systems. By enabling crashtests, which check how the software behaves under extreme conditions, and re-enabling various mochitests (which ensure that smaller parts of the browser work properly), the team aims to enhance the overall stability of WebRTC, the technology that supports real-time video and audio communication in the browser. These changes are especially relevant for macOS users, ensuring that features like screensharing are robust and function as intended. **This work is crucial because it helps maintain a smooth experience for users engaging in video calls and shared activities online.**
